findings.title: Need for Notification Priority Levels
findings.description: Users require a system to distinguish between critical alerts and routine updates, as all notifications currently appear with the same weight, leading to important messages being missed.
assumptions: Users are overwhelmed by the current notification system.
A tiered priority system would improve user experience.

evidence.excerpt: Users need a way to distinguish between critical alerts and routine updates.
Sarah described the experience as 'drowning in noise'... transactional alerts carry the same visual weight as critical incidents.

recommendations: Implement a three-tier priority system for notifications.

findings.title: Desire for Intelligent Notification Filtering
findings.description: Power users express a willingness to pay for a tier that offers intelligent filtering of notifications to reduce noise and improve focus.
assumptions: Users are willing to invest in features that enhance their productivity.

evidence.excerpt: She would pay more for a tier that offers intelligent filtering.

recommendations: Explore premium features that include intelligent notification filtering.

findings.title: Need for Granular Notification Preferences
findings.description: Admins require the ability to configure granular notification preferences, allowing defaults per team and individual overrides to cater to diverse user needs.
assumptions: Different teams have varying notification needs.

evidence.excerpt: Her top request is granular notification preferences — she needs to configure defaults per team.

recommendations: Develop a user preference page for notification categories.

findings.title: Mobile Notification Reliability Issues
findings.description: Casual users report significant issues with mobile notification delivery, particularly on Android 14 devices, leading to frustration and potential churn.
assumptions: Mobile reliability is crucial for user retention.

evidence.excerpt: Several users on Android 14 report that push notifications silently fail.
Marcus mentioned he almost switched to Competitor A's product last month because their mobile reliability is better.

recommendations: Prioritize fixing mobile notification delivery issues on Android 14.

findings.title: Accessibility Concerns with Notification Center
findings.description: Users with accessibility needs face challenges navigating the notification center, particularly those using screen readers and colorblind users struggling with color-only indicators.
assumptions: Accessibility features are essential for inclusivity.

evidence.excerpt: Several team members using screen readers have difficulty navigating the notification center.

recommendations: Enhance accessibility features in the notification center.

summary: The analysis reveals critical insights into user needs for SmartNotify, particularly around notification prioritization, filtering, and mobile reliability. There is a strong demand for granular preferences and accessibility improvements, indicating significant opportunities for feature enhancements and user retention strategies.
